sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, values_list LIST<INT>, timestamp TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, [1, 2, 3, 4, NULL]), (2, [5, 6, 7, 8, 9]), (3, [NULL, NULL, NULL]);
SELECT id, array_reverse(values_list) AS reversed_list FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE values_list IS NOT NULL);
SELECT id, array_reverse(values_list) AS reversed_list, timestamp - INTERVAL '1 DAY' AS adjusted_timestamp FROM test_table WHERE array_reverse(values_list) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, count(*) AS occurrences FROM test_table WHERE array_reverse(values_list) IS NOT NULL GROUP BY id HAVING occurrences > 0;
CREATE TABLE additional_table (id INT, start_time TIMESTAMP, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO additional_table VALUES (1, '2023-01-01 00:00:00', INTERVAL '5 HOUR'), (2, '2023-01-01 06:00:00', INTERVAL '1 HOUR');
SELECT a.id, array_reverse(a.values_list) AS reversed_list, a.timestamp - b.duration AS end_time FROM test_table a JOIN additional_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE end_time > '2023-01-01 05:00:00';
SELECT DISTINCT id, array_reverse(values_list) AS reversed_list FROM test_table WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM additional_table);
WITH NewData AS (SELECT id, array_reverse(values_list) AS reversed_values FROM test_table WHERE values_list IS NOT NULL) SELECT * FROM NewData WHERE reversed_values IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, array_reverse(values_list) AS reversed_list FROM test_table WHERE (array_reverse(values_list) IS NOT NULL OR timestamp IS NULL) AND id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
